series_description: Audits and reviews performed on the agency, including the working papers that support the audit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
The State Auditor's Office retains any
copies of its audits performed on Texas
state agencies.

series_description: Including any supporting documentation created and/or used to justify and support legislative appropriations request by an agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version of
Legislative Appropriation Requests
must be submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program per 13
TAC 3.3(a)(3)(A). Working files and
related documentation used in creating
the final plan are not subject to archival
review and may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period

series_description: Complaints and other unsolicited feedback received by an agency from the public concerning the agency and records pertaining to the resolution of complaints.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: If a complaint becomes the
subject of litigation, it must be included
in and is subject to the minimum
retention period of item number
1.1.048.

series_description: Any document that officially initiates, rescinds, or amends general office procedures.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description: Calendars, appointment books or programs and scheduling, or itinerary records maintained by staff during business hours that document appointments, itineraries, and other activities of agency officials or employees.
retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 1 year
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the calendars,
appointment, and itinerary records of
elected officials, executive staff, board
or commission members, division
directors, and program heads require
archival review. Contact the State
Archives when these records have met
their retention periods. CAUTION: A
record of this type purchased with
personal funds, but used by a state
official or employee to document his or
her work activities may be a state
record and subject to this retention
period. See Open Records Decision 635
issued in December 1995 by the
Attorney General

series_description: Includes all correspondence and documentation relating to requests for records that are furnished to the public under Public Information Act (Chapter 552
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
legal_citation: Government Code). Also includes withdrawn requests.

series_description: Includes all correspondence and documentation relating to requests for records that are excepted under the Public Information Act (Chapter 552
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
legal_citation: Government Code), including records provided to the legislature under section 552008

series_description: Copies of all open meetings notices; or any other documents required by law to be submitted to the Texas Register.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description: Notes or text of speeches, papers, presentations, or reports delivered in conjunction with agency work.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches,
papers, and presentations given by or
on behalf of executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors and program heads require
archival review

series_description: Records created by or on behalf of an agency in anticipation of or in the adjudication of a lawsuit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTES: Cases that set legal
precedent or exhibit historical value
will be evaluated by the Archives and
Information Services Division of the
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission for archival preservation.

series_description: Operational strategic plans, which include descriptions of information resources programs, prepared in accordance with
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version of
Strategic Plans must be submitted to
the Texas State Publications Depository
Program per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(C).
Working files and related
documentation used in creating the
final plan are not subject to archival
review and may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.
Only charts showing the overall
arrangement and administrative
structure of the state agency need to
be transferred to the Archives and
Information Services Division, Texas
State Library and Archives Commission.
Organizational charts showing
division/department level detail are not
considered archival.
This series from 1.1.055 & 1.1.023.
legal_citation: Government Code, 2054.095 and 2056.002. Includes working files and related documentation used in creating the final plan.

series_description: Self-evaluations and plans documenting compliance with the requirements of the Americans With Disabilities Act.
retention_statement: 3 years

series_description: Minutes or notes, and supporting documentation, taken at internal agency staff meetings.
retention_statement: 1 year

series_description: Annual report to the Governor and Legislature as required by the agency's enabling statutes. Includes working files and related documentation used in creating final report
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version of
these agency reports must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program per 13
TAC 3.3. The requirement is met by
sending the required copies of the
reports to the Texas State Publications
Depository Program, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.
Working files and related
documentation used in creating the
final report may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.

series_description: Annual, sub-annual, or special reports or studies on non-fiscal aspects of an agency's programs, services, or projects compiled by agency personnel, or by consultants under contract with an agency that are not noted elsewhere in this schedule. Includes reports distributed either internally or to other entities.
retention_statement: 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Reports are archival
when they deal with significant aspects
of the agency's programs. For agencies
that have had an archival appraisal,
separate this records series by each
type of archival coding, A, R, or E. See
page ix for more information.

series_description: Reports made to the Office of Attorney General (OAG) on an agency's Public Information Act activities.
retention_statement: 2 years

series_title: Sunset Review Report and Related Documentation
series_description: Sunset Review Report, agency self-study, and other correspondence and supporting documentation related to the Sunset review process for state agencies
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description: Final agreement described by
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
legal_citation:
Texas Civil Practice and
Remedies Code,
Chapter 154.071; Government Code 2009.054(c), associated with a matter conducted under an alternative dispute resolution procedure in which personnel of a state agency participated as a party on the agency's behalf

series_description: Agency copy. A records retention schedule (i.e. form SLR 105) that identifies the records that are created and maintained by an agency, provides the minimum timeframes the records must be retained, and includes instructions for their disposition. This series may include working files and documentation of certification and approval by the Texas State Library and Archives Commission
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
Original is retained for 50 years by the
State and Local Records Management
Division, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.

series_description: Forms used to track the transmittal of records to/from onsite and offsite storage areas or a transfer of physical custody
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description: Logs or similar records listing records disposed of by an agency, which might include records series title, dates of records, and date of disposition.
retention_statement: 10 years
comments:
CAUTION: Disposition can mean
destroyed or transferred.

series_description: Agency copy of form RMD 106 granting a state agency the authorization to store records at the State Records Center.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description: Worksheets or working papers used to capture records inventory information, including location tracking and to document existence and requirements of a records series.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description: Records management plans and similar records that establish the policies and procedures under which records and information are managed in an agency
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description: Transmittals and related service documentation (e.g. form RMD 109) for disaster recovery services provided by TSLAC or other entities
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description: Forms used by TSLAC (e.g. form RMD 113) or other entities to establish disaster recovery services, authorize agency staff to access the media, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description: Biennial information security plan for protecting the security of the agency’s information.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description: Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters of reference, and similar documents whose submission by candidates for vacant positions is required on the application form, by application procedures, or in the employment advertisement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: Agency copies of information relating to the selection by employees of life, disability, health, vision, dental, disability, and other types of insurance or benefits offered by the State of Texas to its employees.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Documents that serve as
payroll deduction authorizations must
be maintained for the retention period
prescribed for item number 3.2.001.

series_description: Internal or external announcements or advertisements of job openings, promotions, training programs, or opportunities for overtime.
retention_statement: 2 years

series_description: Includes notes of interviews with candidates, questions asked of applicants, and all other records that document the selection process.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description: Job evaluations, performance appraisals, or other similar documents used to evaluate the performance of an employee
retention_statement: 2 years

series_description: Corrective actions are those actions which do not affect pay, status, or tenure and are imposed to correct or improve an employee's job performance. Disciplinary actions are those actions which may affect pay, status, or tenure. They are imposed to discipline an employee whose conduct is harmful to the best interests of the state, the agency, or the employee work force; and for failure to improve performance or conduct following imposition of corrective action. May include cause for demotion, suspension, or dismissal, and the reasons for failure to give written notice of resignation. May also be used to document evidence of employee self- improvement efforts, as well as favorable and unfavorable communication.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: Forms or similar records used to create or change information concerning the records of an employee including pay grade, position classification, employee number, evaluation date, and termination of employment.
retention_statement: 2 years

series_description: Job descriptions, including all associated task or skill statements, for positions in the agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description: Certificates of completion, certifications, licenses, transcripts, test scores, or similar records documenting the training, testing, certification, licensing, or continuing education achievements of an employee.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: Federal reporting form (INS I-9).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description: §552024 Agreements between employee and agency authorizing certain actions, including acknowledgement forms, telecommuting agreements, outside/secondary employment authorizations, or other documentation that show proof of receipt and awareness of and adherence to agency policies and procedures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description: Employee or applicant requests for reasonable accommodation under the ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description: Documentation used to start, modify, or stop all voluntary or required deductions from payroll, including garnishment or other court- ordered attachments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description: Payroll records and registers documenting employee earnings, wages, and pay. This may include but is not limited to payroll input records, summary statements, payroll vouchers, payroll detail sheets, and payroll history
retention_statement: 4 years

series_description: Includes 1099, W2, FICA and other tax records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description: Used to make increases or decreases to employees' gross pay, FICA, retirement, or in the computation of taxes.
retention_statement: 2 years

series_description: Employer's copy of "Employee's Withholding Exemption Certificate."
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description: Records and documentation relating to unemployment compensation claims, including reimbursement of funds disbursed by Texas Workforce Commission (TWC).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: Forms used to deposit employee’s earnings into a specified personal account.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description: Records documenting the amount of pension or deferred compensation earned by individual employees
retention_statement: 4 years

series_description: Affirmative action plans for both regular employees and apprenticeship programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: Affirmative action plans for apprenticeship programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description: Reports providing statistical information on labor force.
retention_statement: 3 years

series_description: Minimum information needed to verify employment. Includes name, social security number, exact dates of employment, last known address; and most recent public access authorization.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 75 years

series_description: Records relating to review and monitoring of job classifications within an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description: Work, duty, shift, crew, or case schedules, rosters, or assignments.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description: Reports to the agency from TWC or its predecessor pertaining to employees.
retention_statement: 3 years

series_description: Any internally distributed manuals, guidlines, or similar records that define agency wide policies and procedures concerning the personnel of an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description: Any reports compiled by an agency on aspects of personnel staffing, including listing of all staff by program or name, staff hired during a month, detailed listings of employees within its organizational structure, position vacancies, analyses of turnover rates and seasonality of employment, etc.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description: Includes documentation used to complete EEO (Equal Employment Opportunity) reports.
retention_statement: 3 years

series_description: Timesheets, leave requests, and overtime authorizations.
retention_statement: 4 years

series_description: Records documenting number of hours transferred in and out of comprehensive sick leave pool
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description: Claims; invoices; statements; copies of checks and purchase orders; expenditure authorizations; ledgers; encumbrance, purchase, general journal, expenditure, and special vouchers; and similar records that serve to document disbursements, including those documenting claims for and reimbursement to employees for travel and other expenses.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description: Internal and external periodic fiscal management reports and associated worksheets, including federal financial reports, salary reports, HUB reports, investment performance reports, etc.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 5 years

series_description: Periodic reports compiled from information entered into the Uniform Statewide Accounting System (USAS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description: Yearly report compiled from information entered into the Uniform Statewide Accounting System (USAS).
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description: Records documenting the internal and external procedural requirements with respect to the accounting department of a state agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description: Documentation relating to the capital and fixed assets of a state agency, including equipment or property history cards containing data on initial cost, depreciation schedules or summaries used for capital outlay budgeting, and property sale, auction, or disposal records of agency owned equipment and property.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years

series_description: Records documenting requests for payment of a sum of money according to the terms of a policy or contract.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description: Records documenting financial obligations of a state agency that are not payable within one year of the date of the balance sheet, including debentures, loans, deferred tax liabilities, bonds, and pension obligations.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description: Signature cards or similar records establishing authority of an agency employee to initiate or authorize financial transactions on behalf of an agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description: Required by the General Appropriations Act. Includes encumbrances and documentation about budget transfers and revisions, as well as detail charts of accounts.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description: Records and reports of postage expenses, including postal meter usage.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description: Liability, theft, fire, health, life, automobile, and other policies for government property and personnel including supporting documentation relevant to the implementation, modification, renewal, or replacement of policies. Executed, renewed, or amended on or after September 1, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description: Liability, theft, fire, health, life, automobile, and other policies for government property and personnel including supporting documentation relevant to the implementation, modification, renewal, or replacement of policies. Executed, renewed, or amended on or before August 31, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description: Any internally distributed manual, guidelines, or similar records that establish standard office procedures for an agency, e.g. agency style manuals, telephone protocols, mail room procedures, print shop and photocopy ordering instructions.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description: List of agency contracts, leases, and agreements, including general obligation, land lease, utilities, and construction contracts
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description: Reports summarizing efficiency of facility space utilization, which may include data on room usage, demand, allocation, and capacity
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description: Documentation of mail returned by the postal or other mail delivery services as undeliverable.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
CAUTION: If corrected address
provided and mail rerouted, maintain
address update documentation for
prescribed retention period.

series_description: Includes bid requisition/authorizations, invitations to bid or propose, bid specifications, awarded and unawarded bids, and bid tabulation /evaluations. Associated with a contract executed, renewed, or amended on or after September 1, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description: Includes bid requisition/authorizations, invitations to bid or propose, bid specifications, awarded and unawarded bids, and bid tabulation /evaluations. Associated with a contract executed, renewed, or amended on or before August 31, 2015.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description: Invalid bids that do not meet agency submission requirements and are not included in bid evaluation process (e.g. withdrawn, missed submission deadline, incomplete submission, etc.)
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description: Log, register, etc., providing a record of purchase orders issued, orders received, and similar data on procurement status.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description: W-9 IRS Form used to request a taxpayer identification number (TIN) for reporting information to the Internal Revenue Service (IRS). This includes W-9 forms received by a state agency from vendors.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description: Plans for evacuation of agency facilities in cases of emergency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description: Logs, registers, or similar records documenting visitors to limited access or restricted areas of agency facilities.
retention_statement: 3 years

series_description: Records relating to the issuance of keys, identification cards, building passes, passwords, signed statements or similar instruments of access to agency or state facilities, equipment or automated systems.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description: Reports of accidents to adults on state property or in any other situation in which the state agency could be a party to a lawsuit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description: Reports of accidents to minors on state property or in any other situation in which the state agency could be a party to a lawsuit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description: Records documenting issuance of parking permits and assignments
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description: Agency records pertaining to utility cases, projects and appeals that OPUC intervened or participated in.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
